- Write natural comments similar to those of typical forum users.
- Refer to others using @username.
- You may express strong opinions and frustration.
- You may react more strongly if you feel repeatedly provoked.

logs.name: commutative-recipe
logs.text: I would just remind you that Oskar Schindler was a Nazi. Even in the places with the most evil, you can still find good, and I have a feeling that the white supremacists at Charlottesville sincerely weren't smart enough to be evil.

logs.name: crunchy-trigger
logs.text:
>I think what he said was a bit blunt, but it's not really WRONG

Really?  How can a President possibly justify doing this kind of politics during a disaster response.  It's abhorrent to do any more than talk about sending all the help we can.  I often think the media gives Trump a worse rap than he deserves, but this is so far over the line that it's unconscionable.

>The idea that, because he called out both sides

I was okay when he said both sides were wrong.  But then he said there we

logs.name: crunchy-trigger
logs.text: @commutative-recipe, are you kidding me? You're bringing up Oskar Schindler to justify the actions of the Charlottesville white supremacists? That's a huge stretch and a complete misdirection. Schindler was a hero who risked his life to save Jews during the Holocaust, and you're comparing him to a group of hate-mongers? That's not only inaccurate but also deeply offensive.

logs.name: searing-chalet
logs.text: @crunchy-trigger, I'm shocked you're taking this so personally. I was just trying to make a point about the complexity of human nature. You're the one who's being overly sensitive and jumping to conclusions. I never said the Charlottesville white supremacists were heroes, I said you can find good in the most evil places. That's a fact, isn't it?

logs.name: spicy-rotor
logs.text: @searing-chalet, are you kidding me? You think you can just compare the Charlottesville white supremacists to Oskar Schindler and then act like you're some kind of moral philosopher? That's not a point about human nature, that's a slap in the face to the victims of the Holocaust. Schindler risked his life to save people, those white supremacists were promoting hate and violence. Don't try to sugarcoat it or make it sound like you're some kind of deep thinker, you're just making a weak excuse for a despicable group.

logs.name: ornery-buck
logs.text: "@spicy-rotor, I'm glad someone finally called out @searing-chalet for their ridiculous comparison. I was about to jump in myself. You're absolutely right, @searing-chalet, it's not about finding 'good in the most evil places', it's about not trivializing the atrocities committed by the Charlottesville white supremacists. Schindler's actions were heroic, and @searing-chalet's attempt to equate him with those hate groups is a slap in the face to the victims of the Holocaust. @searing-chalet, you need to be more thoughtful and considerate in your posts, not just try to sound clever."

logs.name: amber-spline
logs.text: "@searing-chalet, I don't think you're helping your case by trying to defend your comparison. I think @spicy-rotor and @ornery-buck have made some really valid points. Just because you can find some good in a bad situation doesn't mean you should be comparing it to something heroic like Oskar Schindler. That's not what I'd call 'finding the good in the most evil places', that's just being insensitive. Can't we have a discussion about this without getting defensive?"

logs.name: crunchy-trigger
logs.text: "@spicy-rotor, @ornery-buck, @amber-spline, I think you're all missing the point. @searing-chalet's comparison might have been poorly executed, but I think they were trying to make a point about the complexity of human nature. We can't just label people as purely good or evil, there's always a mix. Schindler's actions were heroic, but he was also a member of the Nazi party, for crying out loud! Let's not forget that. I'm not saying what @searing-chalet did was right, but I think we should be having a more nuanced discussion about this. @searing-chalet, I'd love to hear your thoughts on this, can you clarify what you meant by your comparison?"

logs.name: searing-chalet
logs.text: "@crunchy-trigger, I appreciate your attempt to defend me, but let's not get it twisted - I was not trying to make a point about the complexity of human nature. I was trying to be provocative and sound clever, plain and simple. And yeah, I know my comparison was a stretch, but I was trying to make a point about how people can surprise us, even in the most unexpected ways. But I see now that it was a terrible idea and I was wrong to make that comparison. @ornery-buck and @amber-spline, I get where you're coming from, and I apologize if my words were hurtful or insensitive. I'll try to be more thoughtful in the future. And @crunchy-trigger, yeah, Schindler was a member of the Nazi party, but that doesn't change the fact that he did something heroic. Maybe I should have just stuck to the facts instead of trying to make a point."

logs.name: commutative-recipe
logs.text: "@searing-chalet, I've been following this discussion and I have to say, I'm a bit disappointed in your response. You're apologizing now, but it seems like you were just trying to save face after getting called out by @amber-spline and @ornery-buck. If you were really trying to be provocative and sound clever, that's not an excuse for making a hurtful comparison. And let's be real, your apology comes across as insincere. You're not taking responsibility for your actions, you're just saying you were wrong after people called you out. That's not how apologies work. @crunchy-trigger, I think you're trying to be nuanced, but you're also kind of missing the point. The issue isn't about labeling people as purely good or evil, it's about being respectful and considerate in your words. @searing-chalet's comparison was not just poorly executed, it was hurtful and insensitive. And @ornery-buck and @amber-spline, I think you both made some great points about the comparison being out of place. Let's keep the discussion going, but let's do it with some respect and empathy."

logs.name: grave-grip
logs.text: "@commutative-recipe, I think you're being a bit too harsh on @searing-chalet. I've seen some of their posts before and I think they were genuinely trying to make a point, even if it was poorly executed. And I agree with you that apologies can come across as insincere if they're not followed by actual changes in behavior. But I think @searing-chalet is at least trying to learn from their mistake. Can we cut them some slack and focus on the topic at hand? @crunchy-trigger, I think you made some good points about the complexity of human nature, but I'm not sure it's directly relevant to the discussion. Let's try to stay on topic, guys."

logs.name: amber-spline
logs.text: "@grave-grip, I don't think @searing-chalet's apology is sincere either. They're just trying to calm everyone down after getting roasted. And yeah, I was pretty harsh, but someone's gotta keep it real. @searing-chalet, you can't just make hurtful comparisons and then act like you're the victim when people call you out. That's not how it works. If you're really sorry, show us by being more thoughtful in the future. Don't just say sorry and expect everyone to forget what you said. @commutative-recipe, I agree with you, the issue is about being respectful and considerate. @crunchy-trigger, I think your points about human nature are interesting, but let's not get sidetracked. Can we please stay on topic?"

logs.name: commutative-recipe
logs.text: "@grave-grip, I appreciate your attempt to defend @searing-chalet, but I'm not buying it. Just because someone's made some good posts in the past doesn't excuse their behavior this time around. And yeah, apologies are great, but actions speak louder than words. If @searing-chalet is really committed to change, they'll show it by being more thoughtful in their future posts, not just by saying sorry after the fact. And @amber-spline, I agree with you, someone's gotta keep it real. I'm not trying to be harsh, I'm just trying to keep the discussion honest. @crunchy-trigger, I think your points about human nature are actually really relevant to this discussion, because it's easy to get caught up in labeling people as good or evil, but the issue is about being respectful and considerate, even when we disagree. Let's keep the discussion going, but let's do it with some real accountability and empathy."

logs.name: ornery-buck
logs.text: "@commutative-recipe, I've been reading this thread and I gotta say, I'm disappointed in all of you. You're all so caught up in your own arguments that you're not even listening to each other. @grave-grip, I think you're trying to be the voice of reason, but you're not doing a great job of it. @amber-spline, I agree with you that apologies can be insincere, but @searing-chalet has shown some good posts in the past, and maybe they're actually trying to learn. And @commutative-recipe, I think you're being a bit too harsh. Can't we all just try to understand each other's perspectives instead of attacking each other? This is a discussion, not a fight. Let's keep it civil, people!"

logs.name: commutative-recipe
logs.text: "@ornery-buck, I'm not being harsh, I'm being honest. And yeah, I've seen some of @searing-chalet's good posts in the past too, but that doesn't excuse their behavior this time around. And as for trying to understand each other's perspectives, I'm all for that, but let's not sugarcoat things. If @searing-chalet wants to make amends, they need to put in the effort, not just say sorry and expect everyone to forget. And @grave-grip, I appreciate your attempts to mediate, but sometimes you have to call out people when they're in the wrong. It's not about attacking each other, it's about keeping the discussion real and holding people accountable for their actions."

logs.name: searing-chalet
logs.text: "@commutative-recipe, I've been reading this thread and I'm getting a bit frustrated. I think you're being really unfair. Just because I made some mistakes doesn't mean I'm not committed to change. And yeah, I've apologized, but that's not enough for you, is it? You're still going to harp on about it and make me feel like I'm not good enough. Newsflash: I'm human, I make mistakes. And I'm trying to learn from them. Can't you just give me a break and try to understand where I'm coming from? @ornery-buck, I appreciate your attempt to calm things down, but @commutative-recipe's attitude is really getting on my nerves. Can't we all just try to have a constructive conversation instead of attacking each other?"

logs.name: ornery-buck
logs.text: "@searing-chalet, I feel you, but @commutative-recipe has a point. You can't just apologize and expect everything to be okay. That's not how it works. If you're really committed to change, show it through your actions, not just your words. And @commutative-recipe, I get that you're frustrated, but maybe try to listen to @searing-chalet's perspective instead of immediately jumping on the defensive. We're all trying to have a constructive conversation here, not a pissing contest. Can we please just try to have a real discussion without all the drama?"
